input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-22T02:40:35.500Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which was removed in MSTest v4. Let me look at their test file to understand what they have and provide the fix.

data.content: MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()`). Here's the fix:
data.reasoningText:
MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` which has been available since earlier versions). Let me update the test file to use the modern pattern.

Actually, in MSTest v4, the recommended replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` which was introduced in MSTest 3.3+. Let me update both the csproj to MSTest v4 and the test file.

toolCalls.name: edit
